title: S&P 500 Market Cap Concentration 2026 — Westmount Research
description: Just 10 stocks now control over a third of the entire S&P 500. See the full weight rankings and how concentrated your index fund really is.

schemas.headline: S&P 500 Market Cap Concentration 2026: How Top-Heavy Is the Index?
schemas.description: 503 S&P 500 companies ranked by index weight. Top 10 stocks control 37.3% of the index. Interactive data, sector breakdown, and concentration analysis.

mainEntity.name: What percentage of the S&P 500 is the top 10 stocks?

acceptedAnswer.text: As of March 2026, the top 10 stocks represent approximately 37.3% of the S&P 500 by market cap weight. The top 5 alone account for 25.1%. This level of concentration is historically elevated.

The S&P 500's concentration is at elevated levels. The top 20 stocks control 47.6% of the index, meaning 4% of companies drive nearly half the returns. This creates risks if large-cap tech declines, as the bottom 400+ stocks only make up ~52% of the index.

mainEntity.name: Which stock has the highest weight in the S&P 500?

acceptedAnswer.text: NVIDIA (NVDA) has the highest weight at 7.08%, followed by Apple (AAPL) at 6.19% and Microsoft (MSFT) at 4.97%. The top 3 stocks alone represent 18.2% of the index.

Market cap weighting means each company's influence on the index is proportional to its total market value. A $4 trillion company like NVIDIA has ~700x more influence than a $6 billion company at the bottom of the index. This is why the S&P 500 can rise even when most stocks fall.

mainEntity.name: Should I invest in equal-weight S&P 500 instead?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF (RSP) gives each stock ~0.2% weight regardless of size. This reduces concentration risk and increases exposure to mid-cap stocks. Equal-weight has historically outperformed during periods when large-cap leadership rotates, but underperforms when mega-caps dominate (as in 2023-2025).
